why: "Financial leagues" is a mistranslation; the ticker lists finance verticals, so "Financial services" is the right UK category.

to: Leads from the calculator land in your CRM with full UTM tagging. Subscription payments flow Stripe → auto-invoice via Xero → entry in your accounting system. The manager gets a WhatsApp alert. Zero leads lost to email spam.

expect: Yes. Stripe for international clients, LiqPay or WayForPay for Ukrainian ones. Recurring billing works out of the box: client pays once, then auto-monthly. 24-hour pre-charge reminders, self-serve pause.
to: Yes. Stripe and GoCardless for cards and Direct Debit, Worldpay where you need it. Recurring billing works out of the box: client pays once, then auto-monthly. 24-hour pre-charge reminders, self-serve pause.
why: Drops the "for Ukrainian ones" framing and UA gateways; uses UK payment options (Stripe, GoCardless Direct Debit, Worldpay).

expect: MEDoc, 1C/BAS, ifin (Ukrainian clients). Xero, QuickBooks, FreshBooks (international). API integration: £500-1200 depending on complexity. If you use a different system — we connect.
to: Xero, Sage, QuickBooks, FreeAgent, plus HMRC Making Tax Digital. API integration: £500-1200 depending on complexity. If you use a different system, we connect.

expect: GDPR-compliant architecture. SSL required. AES-256 encrypted databases. 2FA access. Regular security audits. PCI DSS for card data — we use Stripe / LiqPay (no card numbers stored locally).
to: GDPR-compliant architecture. SSL required. AES-256 encrypted databases. 2FA access. Regular security audits. PCI DSS for card data: we use Stripe and GoCardless, so no card numbers are stored locally.
why: Plain UK GDPR is fine; swaps LiqPay for GoCardless and reworks the em dash per the style guide.

expect: Yes. MEDoc integration (for Ukrainian sole proprietors/LLCs) or Stripe Invoicing (international) generates invoices after payment. PDF with your legal name, address, tax ID. The client gets it by email + via the client portal.
to: Yes. Xero integration or Stripe Invoicing generates invoices after payment, whether you're a sole trader or a limited company. PDF with your legal name, address and VAT number. The client gets it by email and via the client portal.
why: Removes MEDoc and the "Ukrainian sole proprietors/LLCs" framing; uses UK legal forms (sole trader, limited company) and VAT number, with Xero as the UK invoicing tool.
